prompt_text:
### Demographics: 67-year-old Asian woman

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 20 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes after hyperglycemia symptoms (polyuria, polydipsia, weight loss)
Current symptoms: Rare nocturnal hypoglycemia (1 episode in 3 months); no DKA/HHS signs; Despite repeated counseling on insulin need, the patient strongly refuses injections; shared decision was made to intensify oral therapy and reassess within 2-4 weeks.

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 3b (30-44)
ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; Coronary artery disease (History of CABG)
Body mass index category: Obese Class I
Diabetic retinopathy
Diabetic neuropathy
Diabetic Retinopathy
Diabetic Peripheral Neuropathy
Diabetic Foot Ulcer / High-Risk Foot
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 3b (30-44); HF (No); ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; MASLD: Normal; BMI Category: Obese Class I
- Family history: No family history of type 2 diabetes (elicited)
- Social history: Never smoker; Alcohol 3-4 drinks/week (2-3 per sitting)

### Medication information
- Current medications: Metformin 500mg twice daily; Empagliflozin 10mg once daily; Glimepiride 2mg once daily; Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily), Finerenone (10 mg daily)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 68.7 kg; Height 165.4 cm; BMI 25.1 kg/m²; Blood pressure 110/66 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 8.0%; 9 months ago 8.5%; 6 months ago 9.0%; 3 months ago 9.5%; current 10.0%), FPG (12 months ago 218mg/dL; 9 months ago 235mg/dL; 6 months ago 190mg/dL; 3 months ago 227mg/dL; current 300mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 69.3kg; 9 months ago 65.2kg; 6 months ago 65.9kg; 3 months ago 65.8kg; current 68.7kg), Height (12 months ago 165.4cm; 9 months ago 165.4cm; 6 months ago 165.4cm; 3 months ago 165.4cm; current 165.4cm), BMI (12 months ago 25.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 24.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 24.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 24.0kg/m²; current 25.1kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 120mmHg; 9 months ago 120mmHg; 6 months ago 120mmHg; 3 months ago 120mmHg; current 110mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 62mmHg; 9 months ago 63mmHg; 6 months ago 65mmHg; 3 months ago 66mmHg; current 66mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 22U/L; 9 months ago 29U/L; 6 months ago 23U/L; 3 months ago 22U/L; current 25U/L), AST (12 months ago 27U/L; 9 months ago 28U/L; 6 months ago 21U/L; 3 months ago 28U/L; current 25U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 0.8mg/dL; 9 months ago 0.9mg/dL; 6 months ago 0.8mg/dL; 3 months ago 0.9mg/dL; current 0.9mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 36mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 32mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 35mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 32mL/min/1.73m²; current 32mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 19mg/g; 9 months ago 28mg/g; 6 months ago 30mg/g; 3 months ago 24mg/g; current 30mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 100mg/dL; 9 months ago 98mg/dL; 6 months ago 93mg/dL; 3 months ago 99mg/dL; current 96mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 37mg/dL; 9 months ago 33mg/dL; 6 months ago 37mg/dL; 3 months ago 37mg/dL; current 35mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 117mg/dL; 9 months ago 119mg/dL; 6 months ago 115mg/dL; 3 months ago 114mg/dL; current 124mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 9.6g/dL; 9 months ago 9.6g/dL; 6 months ago 10.2g/dL; 3 months ago 10.3g/dL; current 10.0g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Not measured (Current); fasting C-peptide Not measured (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Very poor adherence (estimated <50%); SMBG rarely performed; lifestyle control severely limited. Strong injection refusal is documented; under shared decision-making, the patient accepts oral regimen adjustment with short-interval reassessment.
Financial context: Patient has comprehensive insurance with minimal out-of-pocket burden and reports no financial barriers to clinically indicated therapies (budget ≤ KRW 1,000,000/month).
